notes: One LIVE aider REPL session that finishes a turn, sits idle PAST readyTTL while its process stays alive, then resumes normally on the next prompt â€” readyâ†’workingâ†’ready, a long idle dwell in ready, then readyâ†’workingâ†’ready again, on a SINGLE live entity (one proc-<PID>). WHY this proves the scenario: aider's PID is discoverable via the /aider command-line match (pid.go), so the daemon's readyTTL sweep (pid_manager.go) first filters stale ready sessions, then explicitly SKIPS deletion when kill(pid,0)==nil â€” the live aider TUI keeps the ready session out of the sweep exactly as it does for claudecode. The recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s on the daemon so the idle_window's 15000ms duration_at_least_ms cleanly exceeds the TTL in ~45s instead of the 30-min production default; the shortened TTL only affects the recording, not production behaviour. If the liveness skip regresses, the idle_window invariants fire (transcript_removed for the live session during idle, or a flicker off ready). WHY interactive (not headless `aider --message`): headless aider is single-shot and exits after one reply, so the process would be DEAD during the idle window â€” the very condition the scenario must exclude. The interactive driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh) keeps ONE aider TUI alive via tmux across both sends and the idle sleep, killing the tmux session only at the very end â†’ the process is genuinely alive throughout the dwell. AIDER TURN-END IS SYNTHESIZED, NOT MARKED: the markdown transcript (.aider.chat.history.md, FilesUnderCWD) has no turn-end marker; a `> Tokens:` line closes one model CALL, and end-of-turn is synthesized OUT-OF-BAND by IdleFlush after aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms) quiet, clamped by the SessionDetector's 5s working-refresh poll, so the EFFECTIVE workingâ†’ready latency after a turn's last line is ~5s. The recipe budgets a ~5s post-turn buffer plus a 20s idle sleep so first_turn_end settles before the dwell window is measured and the 20s sleep clearly exceeds the 15s TTL. LAZY TRANSCRIPT: .aider.chat.history.md materializes only once the first user input lands, so the live REPL is observable from launch as a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready (the process scanner) BEFORE the per-cwd transcript appears â€” session_birth (asserting only 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch') legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN/IDLE arcs must all land on the session that goes working: first_turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (the pre-session never enters working); the pid_bind anchors to recording start too (aider binds its PID at REPL birth via the /aider command-line match, BEFORE the first turn â€” same shape as aider/basic-turn â€” so anchoring it to first_turn_start would search past the already-fired pid_discovered), and every turn/idle phase plus the pid_bind pin to first_turn_start's session via same_session_as (FilesUnderCWD keys the whole live entity by one proc-<PID>, so the birth pre-session, the working turns, and the bound PID are all that same session), so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance). Stable structurally: readyâ†’workingâ†’ready, a â‰Ą15s ready dwell with no removal or flicker, then readyâ†’workingâ†’ready again on the same session, final ready. NOTE: this spec is the DOCUMENTED TARGET â€” recording is deferred until aider auth is available in-env (no provider key present at authoring time; headless aider hangs on OpenRouter OAuth).

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (the proc-<PID> pre-session; the lazy transcript has not materialized yet)
First user prompt â†’ working (binds the live aider session; the pre-session never enters working)

PID bound to the live aider session at REPL birth (aider binds its PID via the /aider command-line match before the first turn, same shape as aider/basic-turn) â€” the binding the readyTTL liveness check polls
First turn completes back to ready (IdleFlush synthesizes turn-end after the prompt goes quiet); state never entered waiting
Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL while its process stays alive; state remains ready throughout the idle window (no removal, no flicker to working or waiting)
Next user prompt after the long idle: ready â†’ working resumes normally on the same live session
Second turn completes back to ready; final session state is ready
